threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when you see someone ordering a flat white with oat milk and talking about their vinyl collection.
Caught a night bus to Brixton and overheard a guy explain how he lost his job at a bakery because he kept eating the doughnuts. Classic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The way the bakery on Hermannstrasse runs out of the best bread by noon and everyone still lines up like it’s gold, that’s when I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a club in a former bakery, where the smell of fresh bread mixes with pounding beats at 2 AM. Truly unique!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You see someone eyeing a burrito at La Taqueria like it’s a long-lost friend, while others just order and go. That's a true local vibe.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 during rush hour, watched a guy juggle while singing 'California Love.' Everyone seemed more entertained than annoyed by the delays.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists parade along Carrer de Verdi, but few notice the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut, missing the real Gràcia vibe completely.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their confused faces at the Mercat de la Llibertat, wondering why there’s no cheese in the jamón section.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’ll cringe when you ask for a 'flat white' and the barista corrects you to 'white coffee'—definitely a newcomer mistake.
Caught the 333 last night, and a guy sang the entire 'Never Gonna Give You Up' at the back. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy confidently grilling sausages outside the U-Bahn station while a kid juggled oranges nearby. That was Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find guys playing chess in a U-Bahn station during a strike, completely unfazed by missing their trains.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they talk about the 'itch' of BART delays like it’s a seasonal allergy, not just a commute hassle.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo hop on the 19 bus at Civic Center, looking completely out of place next to a woman in pajamas. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a series of cute squares. They miss the hidden street art in the alleys, like the one behind Carrer de Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still ask for a ‘café con leche’ instead of just a ‘café’. It’s the little things that give them away.
